individuals from McMaster were also award Nobel Prize Physics, faculty member Bertram Brockhouse 1994 work neutron scattering; alumna Donna Strickland 2018 work laser physics.

McMaster University (commonly referred McMaster Mac) public research university Hamilton, Ontario, Canada.
main McMaster campus hectares (300 acres) land near residential neighbourhoods Ainslie Wood Westdale, adjacent Royal Botanical Gardens.
operates academic faculties: DeGroote School Business, Engineering, Health Sciences, Humanities, Social Science, Science.
member U15, group universities Canada.
ACU, ATS, AUCC, CARL, COU, CUP, Fields Institute, IAU, U15.
university bears name William McMaster, prominent Canadian senator banker bequeathed C$900,000 founding.
incorporated under terms Legislative Assembly Ontario 1887, merging Toronto Baptist College with Woodstock College.
opened Toronto 1890.
Inadequate facilities gift land Hamilton prompted relocation 1930.
Baptist Convention Ontario Quebec controlled university until became privately chartered, publicly funded institution 1957.
McMaster University co-educational, over 25,000 undergraduate over 4,000 post-graduate students.
Alumni former students reside across Canada countries.
athletic teams known Marauders, members Sports.
Notable alumni include government officials, academics, business leaders, Rhodes Scholars, Gates Cambridge Scholars, Nobel laureates.
university grew, McMaster Hall started become overcrowded.
suggestion move university Hamilton first brought student Hamilton native 1909, although proposal seriously considered university until years later.
1920s, after previous proposals between various university staff, Hamilton Chamber Commerce launched campaign bring McMaster University Hamilton.
issue space McMaster Hall became more acute, university administration debated future university.
university nearly became federated with University Toronto, been case with Trinity College Victoria College.
Instead, 1927, university administration decided move university Hamilton.
Baptist Convention Ontario Quebec secured $1.5 million, while citizens Hamilton raised additional $500,000 help finance move.
lands university buildings were secured through gifts from graduates.
Lands were transferred from Royal Botanical Gardens establish campus area.
first academic session Hamilton campus began 1930.
McMaster's property Toronto sold University Toronto when McMaster moved Hamilton 1930.
McMaster Hall home Royal Conservatory Music.
Hamilton Hall constructed 1926 preparation university's move Hamilton.
McMaster's oldest buildings examples Collegiate Gothic architecture, with architectural elements such carved ornamentation, bas-reliefs, recessed arched entryways, ashlar found throughout these buildings.
McMaster University city Hamilton, Ontario, Golden Horseshoe along western Lake Ontario.
main campus bordered north Cootes Paradise, extensive natural marshland, east west residential neighbourhoods, south Main Street West, major transportation artery.
northern boundaries popular destination walkers many trails that connect campus Royal Botanical Gardens.
While main campus 152.4 hectares (377 acres), most teaching facilities centered within core 12.1 hectares acres).
addition main campus Hamilton, McMaster owns several other properties around Hamilton, well Burlington, Kitchener, Catharines, Ontario.
university owns manages buildings, both campus.
buildings McMaster vary age, with Hamilton Hall opening 1926, university's Bertrand Russel Archives Research Centre, which opened June 2018.
McMaster main campus divided into three main areas: Core Campus, North Campus West Campus.
Core Campus where majority university's academic, research residential buildings located while North Campus made university's athletic precinct small number surface parking.
West Campus least developed area main campus, containing only buildings, surface parking, undeveloped land.
Panoramic view McMaster University's main campus from southwest, taken June 2008. majority university's facilities located this campus.
University Hall oldest facilities still used university.
university's campus gone through continuous development since 1928. main campus's original buildings Collegiate Gothic architecture, designed William Lyon Somerville, also laid initial campus plan.
They flanked over fifty structures built predominantly from 1940s 1960s.
largest facility McMaster University Medical Centre, multi-use research hospital that home second-largest neonatal intensive care unit third-largest child youth mental health unit country.
connected Life Sciences building Michael DeGroote Centre Learning Discovery, which houses many well-funded research groups areas genetics, infectious diseases, several specific conditions.
McMaster Museum holds highest attendance figures museum Canada.
university's library system member organizations, including Association Research Libraries.
Around million articles were downloaded from library system's electronic journal collection during 2016 academic year.
university library employs total professional, support staff.
library's resource expenditure 2016-2017 academic year approximately $11.5 million, with cent budget allocated serial e-resource subscriptions, cent hard copy acquisitions, cent membership collections support.
library system include four libraries housing 1,274,265 paper books 3,689,973 total resources, including videos, maps, sound recordings, microfilm.
Mills Library houses humanities social sciences collections, with wide range print digital resources.
Innis Library houses content which supports academic research interests DeGroote School Business.
Thode Library houses academic material various disciplines science engineering, while Health Science Library houses books pertaining medical sciences.
